title: solanum sylvaticum
intro: Solanum sylvaticum, also known as wild black nightshade or deadly nightshade, is a poisonous plant species native to the Mediterranean region. While it contains toxic alkaloids such as solanine, it has also been used in herbal medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, although its use should be strictly supervised due to its toxicity. In herbal preparations, Solanum sylvaticum is typically used in small amounts and often in combination with other herbs, such as calendula and chamomile, to create topical creams and ointments for skin health.
